text.title: Semantic Token Layer and Mode Structure
text.summary: Three-tier naming, mode matrix structure, alias rules, and contrast pair co-location requirement for token systems that support theming.

rule.statement: Use three tiers exactly — primitive, semantic, component — and ensure component tokens reference semantic tokens, never primitives.
rule.rationale: Component-to-primitive coupling means every primitive rename requires touching component definitions; the semantic layer is the decoupling boundary.

list.item: Primitive: `color.blue.500 = #3B82F6` — raw value, no design intent.

example.note: Semantic alias with mode values in Figma Variables format

rule.statement: Every alias must have a value in every declared mode; missing values cause silent fallbacks that ship wrong colors.
rule.rationale: A mode with a missing alias resolves to undefined behavior; different platforms handle undefined differently, making the regression unpredictable and hard to reproduce.

rule.statement: Reserve one mode per axis as the explicit default; document inheritance rules so consumers of unset modes fall back deterministically.
rule.rationale: Implicit inheritance from an undocumented default causes surprise regressions when the default mode is renamed or reordered.

rule.statement: Bound mode combinations by design decision, not by combinatorial exhaustion; mode explosion (4 color × 3 density × 5 brands = 60 configs) kills maintainability.
rule.rationale: Each supported configuration requires validation; unchecked mode growth creates an exponential testing burden that makes additions prohibitively expensive.

rule.statement: Co-locate contrast partner pairs in the token spec (e.g., `text.on-surface-primary` declares its surface partner); validation requires both halves.
rule.rationale: WCAG contrast must be verified as a pair, not per token; without co-location the validator cannot determine which text color applies to which surface color in each mode.

rule.statement: Generate runtime CSS custom properties at build time, not at app boot; boot-time JS theming causes flicker and hurts Core Web Vitals.
rule.rationale: CSS custom properties set at boot add a render-blocking JS execution step before first paint; build-time generation eliminates this entirely.

antipattern.description: Per-platform deltas (iOS 14px button padding) expressed as inline overrides on a shared token (`"ios": "14px"`).
antipattern.reason: Inline platform overrides are invisible to designers in Figma and to linters; use separate platform-prefixed tokens instead.
